commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=55bad90467bcf286c2262b6f39b9ccef34ecad05)
commands.review_summary:
The PR moves shell-completion doctor lint and repair into the internal runnable health-check flow while preserving the public registry list/get surface.

PR surface: Source +205, Tests +376. Total +581 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blocker: the public API leak is source-reproducible from the PR diff because `DoctorLintRunOptions` is exported through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/health`. Not applicable as a standalone user bug reproduction.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor and maintainer action: restore the public SDK boundary and add real shell-completion doctor proof; no automated repair lane is selected because contributor real-behavior proof is a merge gate.

target.title: Doctor: structure shell completion repair
target.body:
## Summary

This PR moves shell completion doctor repair onto the internal runnable health-check path while keeping the existing public health-check surface normalized.

The rule remains one flow: check shell completion status, return lint findings when repair is false, and run the existing repair actions when repair is true. `doctorShellCompletion()` stays as the positional presentation wrapper and now prints the structured warnings/changes returned by that flow.

Key behavior:

- `openclaw doctor --lint` can report slow dynamic completion profiles and missing completion cache as structured findings.
- `openclaw doctor --fix` still repairs stale dynamic completion profiles and missing caches from the shell-completion position in doctor order.
- Optional missing shell completion remains quiet in lint/non-interactive detection and still prompts only in repair mode.
- Runnable checks are normalized at registry boundaries so public `openclaw/plugin-sdk/health` list/get consumers still receive detect-capable registered checks.

## Verification

Behavior addressed: shell completion doctor repair now participates in structured doctor lint/repair without exposing a public run-only SDK contract.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 worktree rebased on latest `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/flows/health-check-registry.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false`
- `bash /tmp/codex-review --mode branch`

Evidence after fix: focused Vitest passed 6 files / 56 tests; core tsgo and core test tsgo both passed; codex-review reported no actionable correctness issues.

Observed result after fix: shell completion stale-cache and slow-dynamic cases are structured findings/repairs; optional install remains prompt-only in repair mode; public registry entries remain normalized.

What was not tested: live mutation of a real user shell profile.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
